## Euphoria / Exhaustion Brake — Implementation Spec (corrected per verifier)

**File to add:** `server/euphoria_brake.py`
**Status:** design, calibrated to 6/25–6/26 semis blow-off. **DO NOT SHIP AS ORIGINALLY SPECIFIED** — the clean-continuation guard is self-defeating and the keystone IV-crush helpers are unbuilt (see §6/§7). Fix those first.
**One-line:** Suppress (or invert to FADE-WATCH) BULL signals when a name is ≥2 ATR over its 20d MA **AND** inside a binary-catalyst window (forward ER in option window, or post-print IV crush) **AND** the tape has actually rolled. Extension alone never brakes.

### 1. The problem (6/25–6/26 post-mortem)
Five independent BULL generators all leaned long into the MU-beat blow-off and stayed long Friday as it reversed: SOE (228 BULL/10 BEAR Thu, 210/13 Fri), structural_turns (1,191/0 Thu, 1,185/0 Fri), king_migration (MAGNET UP into a falling tape), flow sentiment (tagged $180M ASK call-buying at the Thursday **high** as BULLISH; $35.2B/78.4% bull complex-wide), net_flow (FLOW_LEADS_UP). None has any awareness of *price extension into a known catalyst*.

### 2. Calibration data (verified to rounding via ThetaData daily EOD, state = Wed 6/24 close → Thu 6/25 open)

| Ticker | Thu-open % vs MA20 | **Thu-open ATR-dist** | Euphoric? |
|---|---|---|---|
| MU | +22.1% | **2.16** | YES (beat blow-off) |
| SNDK | +21.9% | **2.10** | YES |
| AMAT | +21.1% | **2.63** | YES |
| LRCX | +15.9% | 1.94 | borderline (just misses, acceptable) |
| SMH | +5.6% | 0.97 | no |
| ASML | +5.2% | 0.87 | no |
| NVDA | −4.6% | −1.28 | no (AI-compute red) |
| QCOM | −1.4% | −0.18 | no |

**2.0 ATR cleanly separates the euphoric memory cohort (MU/SNDK/AMAT) from everything else** — verifier reproduced the table and confirmed the separation. (KLAC showed a 7:1 split artifact in the verifier's pull and is unusable for calibration, but it is not load-bearing for the separation.) The 2.0-ATR / 6%-IV-crush *numbers are defensible*; the failure modes below are in the catalyst breadth and the guard logic, not the extension calibration.

IV-crush confirmation (MU snapshot IV): 109.8 (Wed pre-print) → 105.8 (Thu) → 96.2 (Fri) ≈ 12.4% relative drop. A 6% relative-drop detector arms the brake the morning **after** a print, which `er_in_window_sync` cannot (it returns False for past ER).

### 3. Thresholds

| Param | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `EUPH_ATR_SUPPRESS` | 2.0 ATR above MA20 | Cleanly separates the 6/25 blow-off cohort; matches 2-sigma stretch convention. |
| `EUPH_ATR_INVERT` | 2.8 ATR above MA20 | Reserve the harder INVERT (→FADE-WATCH) for genuine blow-off extremes; keeps INVERT rare. |
| `EUPH_PCT_FALLBACK` | +18% above MA20 | Degraded closes-only path when ATR unavailable; ~18% ≈ 2 ATR for these high-ATR names. |
| `CATALYST_PRE_DAYS` | 2 days (floor) | Matches existing `er_blocks_long_premium` DTE≥2 convention. |
| **`CATALYST_PRE_DAYS_MAX`** | **5–7 days (NEW CAP)** | **Verifier fix:** bound the pre-catalyst window above. The losing pattern is over-extension INTO an imminent binary, not a month before it. Use `min(dte, CATALYST_PRE_DAYS_MAX)` — OR only arm when ER falls in the **last third** of the option's life. |
| `IVCRUSH_DROP_PCT` | 6% relative IV drop vs 1–2d prior | Catches MU's post-print crush by Friday without firing on daily noise. |
| `IVCRUSH lookback / recent-ER` | 2 sessions IV lookback, ER within last 3 sessions | Bounds the post-print window. |

### 4. The gate
`euphoria_state(ticker, spot, dte, expiration, *, iv=None, signal_dir="BULL") -> EuphoriaVerdict{verdict, atr_dist, pct_above_ma20, catalyst_in_window, iv_crush, tape_rolled}`

Three required axes (AND):
1. **Extended:** `(spot − MA20)/ATR ≥ 2.0` (or `+18%` on the degraded closes-only path).
2. **Catalyst:** forward ER within `min(dte, CATALYST_PRE_DAYS_MAX)` (≥`CATALYST_PRE_DAYS`) days **OR** post-print IV crush (IV down ≥6% rel vs 1–2d ago AND ER printed in last 3 sessions).
3. **Tape has rolled (NEW — verifier-mandated):** lower-high OR a close below the prior session's close OR spot below the session open. **An up-continuing tape is NEVER braked, regardless of IV.** This replaces the original "IV not crushing" exclusion (see §4a).

**Verdict → action per family:**

| Family | Hook (file:line) | SUPPRESS action | INVERT (≥2.8 ATR) action |
|---|---|---|---|
| SOE | `signals.py:2461` (+ veto booster @2388, auto-trade @2326) | should_push=False, DB+UI kept | sig['_euphoria_fade']=True, push FADE-WATCH not BUY |
| structural_turns | `structural_turn.py:1187` preflight → tier '—' @246 | qualified=False | (same; no fade tag → '—') |
| king MAGNET UP | `king_migration.py:633` | downgrade to info-only | info-only |
| flow sentiment | `flow_alert_filter.py:329` `_prefilter` | drop/flag BULLISH call alert | flag as FADE |
| net_flow | `net_flow_signals.py:~662` | suppress FLOW_LEADS_UP | suppress |

All hooks keep DB + UI writes (audit trail); only Telegram/auto-trade are gated — exactly how `is_high_score_fade` and the SOE_A demote already behave.

### 4a. The clean-continuation guard — REDESIGNED (this was the verifier's headline failure)

**The original guard was self-defeating.** It required "IV NOT crushing" to PASS a continuation — but a post-earnings runner BY DEFINITION has crushing IV (that's the post-print regime). So it would have braked exactly the winners it claimed to protect.

**Smoking-gun counter-example (verifier ran the mandated replay — it FAILS for ARM):** Post-print ARM crossed 2 ATR on 5/20 (close 256.73, 2.25 ATR) and **sustained 2.25–5.41 ATR for two-plus weeks while running +18% to +49% forward-5d**. Its IV crushed 136.3 (5/23) → 100.2 (5/26), a −26% relative drop, *while at 4.16 ATR and about to run +25% more*. The original brake would have SUPPRESSED/INVERTED 2 grade-A ARM bull fires on 5/26 right before a +25% run. AMD shows the same sustained 2.2–2.9 ATR pattern; MU/SNDK themselves ran +25%/+38% fwd-5d while >2 ATR extended across 4/29–5/04, saved here only *incidentally* by DTE (their fires used 7–11 DTE and ER was 28+ days out, so the pre-catalyst axis didn't arm — luck, not design).

**Replacement guard (`_clean_continuation_guard`):** PASS (do not brake) when the tape is **up-continuing regardless of IV** — define as: N consecutive higher daily closes (N≈2–3) AND spot holding above a fast MA (e.g. EMA9). The IV condition is removed entirely. The brake only fires once the tape has actually rolled (axis 3 above: lower high / close below prior day / below session open). On 6/25 MU/SNDK both round-tripped off their opening highs by the afternoon fires, so the rolled-tape axis IS satisfied and the Thursday fires are correctly braked — without nuking ARM-style runners that keep making higher closes.

### 5. False-positive guards
- **Catalyst required.** Extension alone never brakes.
- **Tape-rolled required (new).** An up-continuing tape passes regardless of IV crush — fixes the ARM failure.
- **Bears never braked** — `signal_dir` gate at the top; the Friday index PUTs (QQQ 708P +1440%, SPY 732P +298%) are structurally exempt. Verifier confirmed.
- **Per-ticker / surgical.** The flow hook calls `euphoria_state` per alert with per-ticker spot; a non-extended ticker (financials/energy <1 ATR) returns PASS. Verifier confirmed surgical-at-ticker-level.
- **Fail-open everywhere** — missing ATR/MA20/IV-history ⇒ PASS.

> **VERIFIER CORRECTION — drop the AEHR example.** The original spec cited AEHR (+171% momentum leader) as the showcase no-catalyst PASS case. **AEHR never exceeded +18%/1.52 ATR over MA20 this period and was at −0.65 ATR going into 6/25** — it never trips the extension axis at all, so it is a *non-test* of the guard. The guard is currently **UNVALIDATED against any name that actually trips extension without a catalyst.** Re-source a real ≥2-ATR, multi-session, no-ER-in-window, no-IV-crush name and confirm it returns PASS before claiming `feedback_analytical_lean` is honored.

### 6. Would-suppress this week
- **SOE:** ~20 of 438 bull fires (MU/SNDK/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML cohort) suppressed/inverted — incl. the canonical MU 13:43+14:01 POST BOTTOM LAUNCH 1280C at the Thu high (both confirmed losers: 1280C entry ~52 → 11 Fri). The other ~418 (broadly distributed, max 5/ticker) PASS. Surgical, not a blanket long-kill.
- **Flow sentiment (highest-volume lever):** the MU 22.0B + SNDK 4.8B euphoric BULLISH-call notional stream (incl. the $180M/$103M/$72M ASK call-buys tagged BULLISH at the 1:43–2:28pm high) gets dropped/flagged.
- **structural_turns:** the MU/SNDK/AMAT/LRCX slice of the 1,191/1,185 all-long wall zeroed.
- **Wrongly-suppressed winners:** ≈ none **for this specific window** — every euphoric memory name reversed (MU −6.7% Fri, SNDK −10.5%, SMH −4.0%); the only winners were Friday index PUTs (bearish, never touched). **Caveat (verifier):** SOE outcome backfill has not run for this weekend (`outcome_price` NULL for all Thu/Fri bull fires), so "essentially none" is asserted from the named MU contracts, not exhaustively measured across all 438 bull fires. And it holds ONLY for 6/25–6/26 — the ARM 5/26 replay shows the brake DOES kill winners in other windows unless the §4a redesign lands.

### 7. Implementation notes / dependencies / risks
- **CRITICAL — six helper functions are UNBUILT.** `recent_er_within`, `iv_n_days_ago`, `snapshot_iv`, `cache_ma20`, `_clean_continuation_guard`, `rts_cache_get` do NOT exist in `server/` (only `er_in_window_sync` and `compute_rts_universe` exist). The keystone IV-crush post-print path is entirely unimplemented. **Build and unit-test all six before any go-live.**
- **ATR is degraded in the live worker cache.** `worker.py:260` passes closes-only ⇒ `_rts.atr` absent ⇒ brake falls back to the coarser +18% pct path. Fix: feed highs/lows OR read `rts._rts_cache` (`compute_rts_universe`, 1h TTL).
- **Post-print coverage rests entirely on the IV-crush detector** (`er_in_window_sync` can't see past ER). Needs ≥1–2 prior snapshot-IV sessions per ticker; cold-start names get no crush arm (fail-open).
- **IV-crush arm must be gated behind price-failing confirmation** (axis 3). Otherwise it is simultaneously the keystone and the biggest false-positive generator — ARM 5/26 crushed IV at 4.16 ATR right before +25% upside.
- **Pre-catalyst window must be bounded** (`min(dte, 5–7d)` or last-third-of-life). Unbounded `max(dte, 2)` would brake a 30–45 DTE bull signal a month before earnings.
- **Amplifier ordering is load-bearing.** The euphoria veto must run *around* `conviction_booster` (`signals.py:2388`) and triple_confluence, or a blow-off A gets force-pushed by trend factors.
- **Re-run the AMD/ARM 5/5–5/6 + NVDA 5/20 replay to GREEN before go-live.** This is no longer a "pre-go-live TODO" — the verifier ran it and it FAILS for ARM as originally specified. It must pass with the §4a redesign in place.
- **Calibrated on one event week.** Defensible by convention, but confirm against prior earnings-blowoff weeks before going live.
